events.title: US and Iran Trade Heavy Strikes, Escalating Regional Tensions
events.desc: The Middle East has experienced its most intense period of conflict since the April ceasefire, with both the US and Iran launching significant missile and drone attacks. These exchanges, particularly around the Strait of Hormuz, threaten to derail efforts for a lasting peace. The renewed hostilities followed an Iranian attack on a commercial vessel in the Strait, which was engulfed in flames and forced its crew to abandon ship.

events.title: Iran Broadens Attacks on US Facilities in the Gulf, Hormuz Tensions Spike Oil Prices
events.desc: US and Iranian forces engaged in substantial missile and drone exchanges over the weekend and into Monday. Iran's Revolutionary Guard claimed to have struck US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, destroyed radar systems in Oman, and hit fuel tanks and ammunition depots at Prince Hassan Air Base in Jordan, in retaliation for US strikes. The heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have led to a rise in oil prices.

events.title: UK Proscribes Iran's IRGC as Terrorist Organization
events.desc: Britain has officially proscribed Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization. This designation makes it a criminal offense to be a member of the group, attend its meetings, or display its insignia in public. The IRGC, already under British sanctions, is an elite force loyal to Iran's Supreme Leader, established after the 1979 revolution.

events.title: Yemen Military Targets Sanaa Airport Runway to Prevent Iranian Plane Landing
events.desc: Yemen's defense ministry announced that its armed forces struck the runway at Sanaa International Airport to prevent an Iranian plane from landing. Sanaa is controlled by the Iran-aligned Houthi group, while the internationally recognized government operates from Aden.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Missile and Drone Strikes Amid Ceasefire Strain
events.desc: The US and Iran are trading missile and drone strikes, putting significant strain on a fragile ceasefire. The IRGC, Iran's Revolutionary Guard, reported attacking sites linked to the US military in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Oman, following the latest US bombardment. This escalation marks a significant deterioration in relations.

events.title: US Forces Target Iranian Sites in Retaliation for Gulf Attacks
events.desc: US military forces have reportedly launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian targets in the Gulf region. This action follows a series of attacks attributed to Iran targeting US military facilities and allies. The tit-for-tat exchange highlights the precarious state of the ceasefire and raises concerns of further escalation.

events.title: Iran Claims Attacks on US Bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, and Radar Systems in Oman
events.desc: Iran's Revolutionary Guards reported launching attacks on US military facilities in Bahrain and Kuwait, destroying radar systems in Oman, and hitting fuel tanks and ammunition depots at Prince Hassan Air Base in Jordan. These actions are presented as a response to earlier US strikes.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Strikes Following Tanker Incidents in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The US military announced it struck missile sites and command centers, while Iran stated it attacked US bases. This exchange of fire follows recent incidents involving commercial tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional tensions.

events.title: US Hits 90 Iranian Targets Amid Renewed Fighting; 14 Killed Since Tuesday
events.desc: CENTCOM reported striking 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of hostilities. The Iranian health ministry indicated that 14 people have been killed since Tuesday, underscoring the significant human cost of the ongoing conflict.

events.title: US Strikes Iran Again, Threatening Further Retaliation
events.desc: President Trump stated that the US will likely conduct further strikes against Iran, emphasizing that any ensuing conflict would be short-lived. This warning was issued during a NATO summit, while Iran issued a statement of a 'fearless' response.

events.title: Iranian Nuclear and Military Damage Revealed Post-Restricted Satellite Images Release
events.desc: Restricted satellite imagery has revealed damage to key Iranian sites, including ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear facilities, and naval bases. The extent of the damage indicates significant impact from recent strikes.

events.title: EU Ministers to Debate Curbing Trade with Israeli Settlements
events.desc: European Union foreign ministers are scheduled to discuss the potential for new measures to restrict trade with Israeli settlements in the occupied West Bank. A confidential paper from the European Commission outlines three options: an import licensing system, prohibitive tariffs, or a complete ban.

events.title: EU Launches $1 Billion Aid Initiative for Gaza
events.desc: The European Commission has initiated a program with 15 partners to provide €883.6 million ($1 billion) in aid to Gaza. The 'Team Gaza Initiative' aims to support early recovery projects for the residents of Gaza, with contributions from twelve European countries, Japan, the World Bank, and the European Investment Bank.
